name: Salvador Dalí

acts.description: Expressed admiration for Franco and made statements sympathetic to Hitler, though the sincerity vs. provocation is contested
Exploitative control of wife and artistic partner Gala, whom he eventually confined to a castle

behavior_summary: The most famous surrealist made statements supporting Franco and Hitler that may have been genuine or may have been provocation. He was also a self-promoter of genius who called himself a genius constantly.

notable_works: The Sacrament of the Last Supper
Dream Caused by the Flight of a Bee
art.summary: The defining face of Surrealism. One of the most recognizable artists in history. His imagery permeated popular culture globally.

verdict.blurb: He said nice things about Hitler and Franco, which his friends attributed to attention-seeking rather than conviction. He may have been a fascist or he may have been a troll — the historical record is genuinely ambiguous. The melting clocks, on the other hand, are unambiguous masterpieces.
